findings.title: Tail output is fully buffered in Python
findings.impact: When an operator passes a large -n value or tails a very large log, capture_output=True stores the entire tail output in memory before anything is printed, adding avoidable latency and risking high memory use or OOM in the CLI process.
findings.fix: Stream the child process output directly to stdout, for example by invoking tail without capture_output and checking only the return code, or by reading from stdout incrementally instead of materializing result.stdout.
